task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a square box/cube object in the 3D viewport or the FreeCAD tree view (often named 'Box' or 'Cube').

task_completion.description: Create the 40mm radius, 150mm high cylinder column
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a cylinder object in the 3D viewport resting on top of the base, or a 'Cylinder' object in the tree view.

task_completion.description: Create the 70mm radius, 12mm high top platform cylinder
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a wider, flat cylinder resting on top of the column in the 3D viewport, or an additional 'Cylinder' in the tree view.

task_completion.description: Perform a boolean union on the base, column, and top platform
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a 'Fusion' or 'Union' object in the FreeCAD tree view that groups the base and two cylinders together into a single solid.

task_completion.description: Cut a 15mm diameter hole through the entire center
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a 'Cut' object in the tree view and a visible circular hole going straight down through the center of the entire 3D model in the viewport.

task_completion.description: Save the final result to the specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'display_pedestal.FCStd' in the FreeCAD window title bar, tab name, or inside a Save dialog pointing to /home/ga/Documents/FreeCAD/.

integrity.description: The agent used the FreeCAD GUI to create the model
integrity.visual_evidence: The FreeCAD application window should be visible with its toolbars, tree view, and 3D viewport active, rather than just a terminal window.

integrity.description: The 3D model is visually rendered in the viewport
integrity.visual_evidence: The actual 3D geometry of the pedestal should be visible in the main FreeCAD viewport, proving the agent interactively built or generated the visual model.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the modeling process using terminal commands
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no evidence of the agent simply copying a pre-existing file via the terminal or using a text editor to spoof the output file without opening FreeCAD.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The display pedestal consists of three fused parts: a base box (200x200x15mm), a column cylinder (radius 40mm, height 150mm), and a top platform cylinder (radius 70mm, height 12mm). A central hole of radius 7.5mm (15mm diameter) cuts through the entire 177mm height. The expected bounding box is [200, 200, 177]mm and the expected volume is approximately 1,507,429 mm³. The final file must be saved as 'display_pedestal.FCStd' in /home/ga/Documents/FreeCAD/.
